machinery_genes.quote: Among the 41 LEE genes, 10 (escR, escS, escT, escU, escC, escJ, escV, escN, escD, and escF) encode proteins conserved among TTSSs
machinery_genes.note: Named as one of the 10 conserved core TTSS structural genes of the C. rodentium LEE. SctR export-gate role from the apparatus brief vocabulary. Mutant DescR scored '- TTSS' in Table 1.

machinery_genes.quote: Among the 41 LEE genes, 10 (escR, escS, escT, escU, escC, escJ, escV, escN, escD, and escF) encode proteins conserved among TTSSs
machinery_genes.note: One of the 10 conserved core TTSS structural genes; SctS export-gate role from apparatus brief. Mutant DescS scored '- TTSS'.

machinery_genes.quote: Among the 41 LEE genes, 10 (escR, escS, escT, escU, escC, escJ, escV, escN, escD, and escF) encode proteins conserved among TTSSs
machinery_genes.note: One of the 10 conserved core TTSS structural genes; SctT export-gate role from apparatus brief. Mutant DescT scored '- TTSS'.

machinery_genes.quote: Among the 41 LEE genes, 10 (escR, escS, escT, escU, escC, escJ, escV, escN, escD, and escF) encode proteins conserved among TTSSs
machinery_genes.note: One of the 10 conserved core TTSS structural genes; SctU autoprotease/switch role from apparatus brief. Mutant DescU scored '- TTSS'.

machinery_genes.quote: However, except for escF, escC, escD, escN, and escV, the assigned function for these genes was based only on sequence homology.
machinery_genes.note: Core conserved TTSS gene; EscC = SctC outer-membrane secretin. This is one of the five esc genes whose function was experimentally supported (not homology-only) in this paper. Mutant DescC scored '- TTSS'.

machinery_genes.quote: Among the 41 LEE genes, 10 (escR, escS, escT, escU, escC, escJ, escV, escN, escD, and escF) encode proteins conserved among TTSSs
machinery_genes.note: One of the 10 conserved core TTSS structural genes; EscJ = SctJ inner ring lipoprotein. Mutant DescJ scored '- TTSS'.

machinery_genes.quote: However, except for escF, escC, escD, escN, and escV, the assigned function for these genes was based only on sequence homology.
machinery_genes.note: Core conserved TTSS gene with experimentally supported function; EscV = SctV major export-apparatus ring (InvA/MxiA family). Mutant DescV scored '- TTSS'.

machinery_genes.quote: However, except for escF, escC, escD, escN, and escV, the assigned function for these genes was based only on sequence homology.
machinery_genes.note: Core conserved TTSS gene with experimentally supported function; EscN = SctN ATPase. Used in this paper as the defining TTS-null control (DescN secretes no proteins). Mutant DescN scored '- TTSS'.

machinery_genes.quote: However, except for escF, escC, escD, escN, and escV, the assigned function for these genes was based only on sequence homology.
machinery_genes.note: Core conserved TTSS gene with experimentally supported function; EscD = SctD inner MS-ring. Mutant DescD scored '- TTSS'.

machinery_genes.quote: However, except for escF, escC, escD, escN, and escV, the assigned function for these genes was based only on sequence homology.
machinery_genes.note: Core conserved TTSS gene with experimentally supported function; EscF = SctF needle subunit. Mutant DescF scored '- TTSS'.

machinery_genes.quote: 13 other LEE genes were needed for TTS (Fig. 3 and Table 1), with 9 (orf2, orf4, orf5, rorf3, rorf8, orf12, orf15, sepQ, and orf29) required for both translocator and effector secretion
machinery_genes.note: sepQ required for both translocator and effector secretion (Table 1 mutant DsepQ = '- TTSS'); SctQ C-ring/EscQ-family role from apparatus brief. EscQ/SepQ is the EPEC/CR C-ring component.

machinery_genes.quote: 4 (orf3, rorf6, orf16, and sepL) affecting translocator secretion preferentially... This result suggests that SepL and Rorf6 may act as a molecular switch controlling secretion hierarchy of translocators and effectors.
machinery_genes.note: SepL = the LEE gatekeeper (SctW/InvE/MxiC-family). Apparatus, not a transcriptional regulator: it controls which substrate class is exported. Mutant DsepL secretes no translocators but increased effectors.

machinery_genes.role: secreted translocator forming the EspA filament conduit to the host membrane
machinery_genes.quote: The secreted proteins consist of effectors as well as translocators (EspA, EspD, and EspB) required for translocating effectors into host cells.
machinery_genes.note: Translocator = apparatus (host-targeting conduit), explicitly distinguished from effectors. EspA forms the filamentous sheath extending the needle. Table 1: 'Secreted protein/translocator'.

machinery_genes.quote: The secreted proteins consist of effectors as well as translocators (EspA, EspD, and EspB) required for translocating effectors into host cells.
machinery_genes.note: Translocator = apparatus; EspD forms part of the translocation pore in the host membrane. Table 1: 'Secreted protein/translocator'.

machinery_genes.quote: The secreted proteins consist of effectors as well as translocators (EspA, EspD, and EspB) required for translocating effectors into host cells.
machinery_genes.note: Translocator = apparatus; EspB forms part of the translocation pore. Has dual translocator/effector activity in the literature but listed here as a translocator per the apparatus brief. Table 1: 'Secreted protein/translocator'.

notes: Citrobacter rodentium possesses a SINGLE type III secretion system, encoded by the LEE (locus of enterocyte effacement) pathogenicity island, of the Esc/Sep (EscC/EscN/EscV...) injectisome family shared with EPEC/EHEC. The benchmark instance is labelled prophage_CRP1 because its two assigned effectors (ROD_12141, ROD_12161) lie in prophage CRP1, but these and all other C. rodentium effectors are translocated by the one LEE-encoded injectisome; there is no separate prophage-encoded apparatus. The CRP1 prophage carries cargo effectors, not a secretion machine. Apparatus genes therefore come from the LEE characterisation paper (separated-paper rule). Deng et al. 2004 (PNAS) systematically mutagenised all 41 C. rodentium LEE genes and defined the apparatus: this is the same C. rodentium LEE present in the ICC168 genome (Petty et al. 2010, J Bacteriol, the genome-sequence reference). Machinery listed: the 10 conserved core esc structural genes (escR/S/T/U/C/J/V/N/D/F) explicitly named in the text, plus sepQ (C-ring), sepL (gatekeeper/secretion switch), and the three translocators EspA/EspB/EspD (apparatus, per the brief). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery: LEE effectors (Tir, Map, EspF/orf18=EspH, orf2/espG=rorf2, etc.), the prophage CRP1 effectors themselves, chaperones (CesT, CesD, CesD2/orf27, CesF/rorf10, Orf3/Orf16 translocator chaperones), and transcriptional regulators (Ler, GrlA/orf11, GrlR). Lower-confidence calls: orf2/orf4/orf5/rorf3/rorf8/orf12/orf15/orf29 and rorf6 are 'required for TTS' in Deng's Table 1 but are not assigned a named conserved apparatus family in the text (several are likely accessory/assembly factors or uncharacterised), so they were NOT promoted to named machinery genes to stay within the 'naming quote' rule. Family tags map to the unified Sct nomenclature in the apparatus brief; EspA/B/D family marked 'translocon' rather than a single Sct letter. All quotes verbatim from the Deng 2004 PMC full text (PMC373508).
